Die redaktionellen Inhalte entstehen ohne Einfluss durch die Gates-Stiftung.Ja. Große europäische Medien wie »The Guardian« und »El País« haben mit »Global Development« beziehungsweise »Planeta Futuro« ähnliche Sektionen auf ihren Nachrichtenseiten mit Unterstützung der Gates-Stiftung aufgebaut.Der SPIEGEL hat in den vergangenen Jahren bereits zwei Projekte mit dem European Journalism Centre (EJC) und der Unterstützung der Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation umgesetzt: die »« über globale Nachhaltigkeitsziele sowie das journalistische Flüchtlingsprojekt »«, in deren Rahmen mehrere preisgekrönte Multimedia-Reportagen zu den Themen Migration und Flucht entstanden sind.Die Stücke sind beim SPIEGEL zu finden auf der .Mutter mit Baby in SüdkoreaPassanten am Berliner MauerwegBeliebteste Babynamen: Sophia löst Emilia abKopfstand an Bord: Ist das ein Traumjob?Angehende Models im Kakuma-Camp
en_title: South Korea: How mothers are pampered after birth
en_content: Welcome to All Good, the SPIEGEL newsletter with only good news. I'm glad you're here. This is a good occasion to recommend a beautiful story of my colleague Katharina Graça Peters. She was traveling in South Korea, more precisely she looked at facilities where mothers relax after the birth of a child, so-called Sanhujoriwon. The tradition in South Korea is that women should recover and have time for salvation and for themselves. It goes without saying that they are entitled to this rest period. In the luxu variant, such mother hotels not only offer underfloor heating, quiet advice and healthy food in the form of Korean seaweed soup or iron-containing beef, but also spa treatments, massages and yoga. If the mother wants to sleep, nurses take care of the newborns. "In these weeks, South Korean women have no obligations, no expectations have to be met. This is worth a lot in a patriarchal society where they often have to meet the high demands of older relatives in families. The time in Sanhujoriwon belongs only to them and their baby," my colleague writes. Of course, in the country with the world's lowest birth rate, females would be lovingly caressed. All this sounds a little like a better world; some speak of a paradise for mothers in South Korea. But it also has its price. The stay at the Heritage Weekbed costs around 18,700 euros for two weeks. Most post-partum centers are operated privately, there are few public. Of these, the cheapest conversion costs about 900 euros for two weeks. In the capital Seoul, families pay an average of 2000 euros. "And yet these mother hotels are not only reserved for the rich," my colleague writes. For the government now grant lush grants, hoping that more couples want to become parents. After the birth, they received more than 2000 euros, plus money from local offices.Katharina himself has three children – and would like to have experienced such a wellness program for mothers after the labor itself. "In South Korea, one finds that every woman deserves this – I would also like to see such an appreciation in Germany," she says. This week, by the way, not only is Mother's Day. On Thursday was Father's Day. In view of the reality in Germany, he sees not only women, but also men in the disadvantage: "In the same way, in which mothers are forced through structural problems such as missing childcare places, fathers are made more difficult to participate in child education. He writes, for example, by a family-fair tax system that favours single earners disproportionately". Overtime would be glorified; the four-day week was branded as a model for sloth. Malte calls for companies and a state that gives parents more scope. "As a mother or father of a small child you are always full-time, seven days a week, 24 hours each. The benefits that parents provide are invaluable.« You should try not to make life unnecessary. Pandemic, price shock and economic flay – Germany has been blowing trampsal for four years. But the 30-59-year-olds have overcome all this according to a new survey. Many are therefore programmers, construction planners, electrical technicians: a study shows where in recent years particularly many future-oriented work and training places have been created. Cannabis has been legal since April 1. Partialization also means that some offences under previous law are no longer criminal offences because the law applies retroactively. Whoever sits a prison sentence because of a cannabis offense can hope for amnesty: at least 125 people have already been released from custody, shows a How to cultivate his curly molluscs? Does every guitarist have to record a blues album? And how to get rid of drug problems? Rocker Slash talks with my colleagues Arno Frank and Andreas Borcholte about masculinity images, stolen plates and his new addiction to instruments. A ranking of the German language company has identified the most popular baby names: In the hit list there are only few changes – but with the girls a change at the top: Sophia releases Emilia with a short lead. Noah's in front of the boys again. My colleague, Kristin Haug, has conducted an interesting interview with the name researcher Knud Bielefeld on the subject and wrote down on the basis of what criteria he creates his hit lists Until now, solar systems were especially interesting for single-family houses – but a change in the law makes his own sunflow easier for home owners. Many employees and middle-aged workers would like to go in another direction. But that's not so easy. Career coach Bernd Slaghuis explains why many Ü50 job changers stand in their way and how to change. Speaking of professional change, would that be something for you? Sail over the world's seas on a 43-metre long luxury yacht – and are still paid for. Sounds like a dream job. Matrose Nicolai Gebhardt tells, My colleague Heiner Hoffman was once again on the road, this time in a refugee camp in Kenya; the people there live in huts. In his history, however, it is not about the war in the neighboring country of South Sudan, before which people have fled – but about a modelcasting. The young women from the Kakuma camp are currently in great demand on the catwalks in Paris and London. Numerous agencies and talent scouts are therefore on the move and address the potential models, show them how to run and posing. Your offers sound tempting; after adventure and great career.Criticians say: The agencies exploit the poverty of the models, profit from their miserable situation. On the other hand, there are few paths from the refugee camp. "And for many, the model life in Europe can be a chance," says my colleague, Heiner. Did you experience something more motivating or entertaining? Send us by email to your very personal good news from the week – what is good to you, what have you experienced, seen, heard? It can be something small or something life-changing. In the next few weeks, we will introduce a return. * Have a nice long weekend! And if you have not yet registered for this new weekly newsletter, you can report it .Under the title »Global Society«, reporters report on injustices in a globalised world, social policy challenges and sustainable development. The reports, analyses, photo tracks, videos and podcasts appear in a separate section in the SPIEGEL's foreign location. title: Gaza-Krieg: USA zweifeln an Kriegsführung von Israel
subtitle: Die Biden-Regierung hat einen Bericht an den US-Kongress übergeben. Darin wird der Verdacht geäußert, dass die israelische Armee in Gaza mithilfe amerikanischer Waffen das Kriegsvölkerrecht verletzt haben könnte.
content: Ein Bericht des US-Außenministeriums legt nahe, dass die israelische Armee beim Einsatz von amerikanischen Waffen im Gazastreifen gegen das Kriegsvölkerrecht verstoßen haben könnte. Der Bericht wurde am Freitag dem US-Kongress vorgelegt. Darin heißt es, aufgrund der Situation in dem Kriegsgebiet sei es schwierig, einzelne Vorfälle zu bewerten oder abschließende Feststellungen zu treffen, es gebe jedoch »genügend gemeldete Vorfälle, die Anlass zu ernsthaften Bedenken geben«.Das Außenministerium habe von mehreren glaubwürdigen Uno- und Nichtregierungsquellen Berichte über mögliche Menschenrechtsverletzungen durch israelische Streitkräfte erhalten, heißt es weiter.  sei in erheblichem Maße auf US-Verteidigungsgüter angewiesen. Deshalb sei es eine plausible Einschätzung, dass das israelische Militär US-Waffen seit dem 7. Oktober in Fällen eingesetzt habe, die »mit den Verpflichtungen des humanitären Völkerrechts oder den bewährten Praktiken zur Minderung ziviler Schäden unvereinbar« seien.Wie die BBC und die »New York Times« berichten, heißt es in dem Bericht allerdings auch, dass der US-Regierung keine Beweise vorlägen, um diese Annahme tatsächlich zu erhärten. Die USA seien nicht in der Lage, bestimmte US-Waffen mit einzelnen Angriffen in Verbindung zu bringen. Man habe aus Israel »glaubwürdige und verlässliche« Zusicherungen erhalten, dass die gelieferten Waffen gemäß der internationalen Gesetzeslage eingesetzt worden seien.Das israelische Militär verfüge über die Erfahrung, die Technologie und das Know-how, um den Schaden für die Zivilbevölkerung so gering wie möglich zu halten, heißt es weiter in dem Bericht, der von den Demokraten im US-Kongress angefordert worden war. Aber »die Ergebnisse vor Ort, einschließlich der hohen Zahl ziviler Opfer, werfen erhebliche Fragen auf, ob das israelische Militär diese Mittel in allen Fällen effektiv einsetzt«.US-Präsident Biden hatte am Mittwochabend. Für einen Großangriff auf Rafah werde die USA keine Waffen liefern.Die US-Regierung hat in den vergangenen Wochen immer deutlicher Kritik an der israelischen Kriegsführung geäußert. US-Präsident Biden sieht sich mit dem wachsenden Unmut über die steigende Zahl palästinensischer Todesopfer und die in Gaza herrschende Hungersnot konfrontiert, die auch auf israelische Beschränkungen für den Transport von Lebensmitteln und Hilfsgütern zurückzuführen ist. Insbesondere durch die israelische Offensive auf Rafah hat sich das Verhältnis zwischen den USA und Israel verschlechtert.Zerstörtes Wohngebäude in Gaza: Die US-Regierung hat in den vergangenen Wochen immer deutlicher Kritik an der israelischen Kriegsführung geäußert
en_title: Gaza War: US doubts about Israel's warfare
en_content: A report by the U.S. Department of Foreign Affairs suggests that the Israeli army might have violated the international law of war when using American weapons in the Gaza Strip. The report was presented to the US Congress on Friday. It states that, due to the situation in the war area, it is difficult to evaluate individual incidents or to make final findings, but there are "comfortably reported incidents that give rise to serious concerns".The Ministry of Foreign Affairs has received reports on possible human rights violations by Israeli armed forces from several credible sources of uno and non-governmental. is heavily dependent on US defence goods. Therefore, it is a plausible assessment that the Israeli military has deployed US weapons since 7 October in cases that are "incompatible with the obligations of international humanitarian law or good practices to mitigate civilian damage".How the BBC and the New York Times report, however, the report also states that the US government does not provide any evidence to actually cure this assumption. The United States is not able to link certain US weapons to individual attacks. The Israeli military has had the experience, technology and know-how to keep the damage to the civilian population as low as possible, according to the report requested by the Democrats in the US Congress. But "the results on the ground, including the high number of civilian victims, raise considerable questions as to whether the Israeli military use these funds effectively in all cases." For a major attack on Rafah, the US will not provide weapons.The US government has expressed a clear criticism of Israeli warfare over the past few weeks. U.S. President Biden is faced with the growing discontent about the increasing number of Palestinian death victims and the famine in Gaza, which is also due to Israeli restrictions on the transport of food and supplies. In particular, the Israeli offensive on Rafah has worsened the relationship between the USA and Israel. In recent weeks, the U.S. government has expressed considerable criticism of the Israeli warfare
